content: Google Play's old 'Designed for Families' program has been folded into the broader Play Families Policy. Any app whose target audience includes children must comply with COPPA (US) and GDPR-K (EU): no precise location access, no transmission of device/advertising identifiers for child users, additional content and ads restrictions. Apps that meet the Families Policy are eligible for the Play Store 'Kids' tab, which is further curated by the human/teacher-reviewed 'Teacher Approved' program (age-appropriateness, quality, enrichment). In Play Console's 'Target audience and content' section, declaring an age group including children triggers these extra policy and content-rating requirements, including tighter ad-content and SDK restrictions.
